title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration · Risk Brief: Named Constants
summary: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates named constants increment in a domain constant and configuration policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name stable domain values by meaning and keep them near their owning policy.
Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
